title: Retry Safety, Replayability, Backoff, Jitter, and Retry Budgets · Go Client Systems · Go Semantics Case: Retryable Classification
summary: A regional control client sees resets, 429, 503, replayable and non-replayable bodies, and nested retry policies during overload. Planned artifact: Add and verify the http go retryable classification increment in a Go 1.26 net/http bounded retry and replay decision engine. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Classify retry candidates from operation semantics, failure phase, response status, caller budget, and server guidance. Demonstrate the result through Go 1.26 net/http, net/url, context, io, crypto/tls, RoundTripper, and httptest evidence.

Report which phase and policy ended an operation without exposing secrets or claiming the remote effect is known. Demonstrate the result through Go 1.26 net/http, net/url, context, io, crypto/tls, RoundTripper, and httptest evidence.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Go Predict Retryable Classification · Theory Retryable Classification
steps.instruction:
theory case — Go fleet evidence model sees one reset, Retry-After:6, then 503 while a nested helper also retries. Prove method and body replayability, cap attempts and elapsed budget, apply deterministic jitter, and prevent duplicate or amplified work. Competency probe: Classify retry candidates from operation semantics, failure phase, response status, caller budget, and server guidance. Demonstrate the result through Go 1.26 net/http, net/url, context, io, crypto/tls, RoundTripper, and httptest evidence.
